extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam skipped between leadout trains in final 5km, avoided chaos in wet technical finale, outsprinted BAUHAUS Phil who did the work for him
tactical_patterns: BENNETT exploited Bora's leadout by wheel-surfing rather than organizing own train, saving energy for final 200m
BAK Lars prioritized intermediate sprint points in early break despite wet conditions favoring position conservation
Trek-Segafredo overcommitted leadout positioning MOSCHETTI but lost organization through final corner at 300m to go
GROENEWEGEN Dylan boxed in at 200m after leading through flam rouge, suggesting wet corners nullify pure speed advantage

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: tactically astute to conserve position without committing to leadout work, strong finisher even when skipping wheels
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: teammates delivered perfect setup but finished second, suggests positioning discipline problems in chaotic finales
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: led at 300m but boxed by 200m, vulnerability when controlling race without numerical superiority
rider_signals.THEUNS Edward: positive: third place without visible leadout support indicates strong independent positioning instincts
rider_signals.MOSCHETTI Matteo: negative: Trek organized for him but lost cohesion through penultimate corner, needs tighter finale coordination
